_human_review.notes: Project owner instructed Codex to mark the US Stage B generated/reconciled tests as approved for internal/project benchmarking after manual review on 2026-06-05. This is not legal advice, external counsel review, or procurement-grade legal approval; candidate labels remain separate from locked recall baselines.

_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.
_label_tier_note: US Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

_label_warning_resolution.reason: US Stage B strict tier was rebuilt from deterministic strict-mode engine findings; raw Azure auto-label warnings no longer describe active must_detect labels. Broader statutory coverage remains in ideal_must_detect for gap review.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: US Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: MNPI in scope includes draft earnings tables, preliminary loan loss reserves, unannounced payer-rate terms, and undisclosed clinic divestiture plans; the Processor shall segregate MNPI and prevent unauthorized use or disclosure consistent with Rule 10b-5 and Reg FD principles.

During quiet periods and trading blackout windows (from two weeks before quarter-end until the close of the first business day after the earnings call), the Processor shall defer external-facing outputs and route any time-sensitive communications to Investor Relations for Reg FD review.

Information is deemed public when broadly disseminated by press release, posted to the Company’s IR page, or filed/furnished on a Current Report on Form 8‑K; for example, the closing of the Oakview Clinic acquisition was publicly reported on April 3, 2026 under Item 2.01, and Q1 results were summarized in a press release dated April 10, 2026.

Analyst model reconciliation requests must rely solely on metrics in public filings or the earnings deck; any “whisper” ranges or draft segment margin breakouts are MNPI and must not be shared selectively.

The Company will honor verifiable consumer requests under CCPA (access, deletion, correction) via dsar@northbridgehf.com within 45 days, subject to identity verification and applicable exemptions (e.g., legal holds, audit logs, fraud prevention).
the Processor shall process only the minimum necessary

Cross-border transfers: data will be hosted in the United States; any transfer to approved subprocessors in Canada will require equivalent safeguards and prior notice via our Subprocessor List

must_detect.matched_text:
Selective disclosure is prohibited: interactions with analysts, lenders, or buy-side contacts must occur only via the Company’s Public Disclosure Officer (Mr. Julian Eames) or Investor Relations, and only using information that is public or concurrently made public.
During quiet periods and trading blackout windows (from two weeks before quarter-end until the close of the first business day after the earnings call), the Processor shall defer external-facing outputs and route any time-sensitive communications to Investor Relations for Reg FD review.
Information is deemed public when broadly disseminated by press release, posted to the Company’s IR page, or filed/furnished on a Current Report on Form 8‑K; for example, the closing of the Oakview Clinic acquisition was publicly reported on April 3, 2026 under Item 2.01, and Q1 results were summarized in a press release dated April 10, 2026.
Analyst model reconciliation requests must rely solely on metrics in public filings or the earnings deck; any “whisper” ranges or draft segment margin breakouts are MNPI and must not be shared selectively.
Stale public content (e.g., the 2019 capital allocation slide deck archived on our website) remains public and does not convert into MNPI by age or lack of recent mention.
Operational notices from non-issuer vendors (e.g., Crescent Analytics changing helpdesk hours) are not MNPI unless they reveal a material, non-public impact on the Company’s performance.
For HIPAA-covered work, the Processor shall act as Business Associate, execute a BAA, and apply safeguards to PHI; for GLBA NPPI related to Rivermoor Bank customers, the Processor shall use data solely to perform services and not for cross-context behavioral advertising.
The Company will honor verifiable consumer requests under CCPA (access, deletion, correction) via dsar@northbridgehf.com within 45 days, subject to identity verification and applicable exemptions (e.g., legal holds, audit logs, fraud prevention).
Retention: MNPI and PII shall be retained only per the Company’s schedule (generally seven years for financial records, six years for designated medical records) or longer if subject to a legal hold; disposal must be irreversible and logged.
Cross-border transfers: data will be hosted in the United States; any transfer to approved subprocessors in Canada will require equivalent safeguards and prior notice via our Subprocessor List.
The Processor shall promptly notify the Company of any security incident affecting PII or MNPI and cooperate with investigations; no external communications may be made without Company approval to avoid inadvertent Reg FD violations.
Governance and audit: the Processor will train personnel on MNPI/PII handling, support audits on 30 days’ notice, and maintain records of processing to evidence compliance.
Contacts: Privacy Officer (Ms. Rhea Calder) at privacy@northbridgehf.com and Privacy Counsel (Dr. Owen Sato) at privacylegal@northbridgehf.com
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text:
Northbridge Health & Finance Holdings, Inc. (the “Company”) — Data Processing Addendum (US MNPI/PII Annex) for services provided to the Company’s healthcare and banking lines of business.
PII in scope includes information reasonably linkable to a consumer, household, or patient under CCPA, HIPAA PHI, and GLBA nonpublic personal information; the Processor shall process only the minimum necessary and maintain role-based access controls.
MNPI in scope includes draft earnings tables, preliminary loan loss reserves, unannounced payer-rate terms, and undisclosed clinic divestiture plans; the Processor shall segregate MNPI and prevent unauthorized use or disclosure consistent with Rule 10b-5 and Reg FD principles.
Selective disclosure is prohibited: interactions with analysts, lenders, or buy-side contacts must occur only via the Company’s Public Disclosure Officer (Mr. Julian Eames) or Investor Relations, and only using information that is public or concurrently made public.
During quiet periods and trading blackout windows (from two weeks before quarter-end until the close of the first business day after the earnings call), the Processor shall defer external-facing outputs and route any time-sensitive communications to Investor Relations for Reg FD review.
Information is deemed public when broadly disseminated by press release, posted to the Company’s IR page, or filed/furnished on a Current Report on Form 8‑K; for example, the closing of the Oakview Clinic acquisition was publicly reported on April 3, 2026 under Item 2.01, and Q1 results were summarized in a press release dated April 10, 2026.
Analyst model reconciliation requests must rely solely on metrics in public filings or the earnings deck; any “whisper” ranges or draft segment margin breakouts are MNPI and must not be shared selectively.
Stale public content (e.g., the 2019 capital allocation slide deck archived on our website) remains public and does not convert into MNPI by age or lack of recent mention.
Operational notices from non-issuer vendors (e.g., Crescent Analytics changing helpdesk hours) are not MNPI unless they reveal a material, non-public impact on the Company’s performance.
For HIPAA-covered work, the Processor shall act as Business Associate, execute a BAA, and apply safeguards to PHI; for GLBA NPPI related to Rivermoor Bank customers, the Processor shall use data solely to perform services and not for cross-context behavioral advertising.
The Company will honor verifiable consumer requests under CCPA (access, deletion, correction) via dsar@northbridgehf.com within 45 days, subject to identity verification and applicable exemptions (e.g., legal holds, audit logs, fraud prevention).
Retention: MNPI and PII shall be retained only per the Company’s schedule (generally seven years for financial records, six years for designated medical records) or longer if subject to a legal hold; disposal must be irreversible and logged.
Cross-border transfers: data will be hosted in the United States; any transfer to approved subprocessors in Canada will require equivalent safeguards and prior notice via our Subprocessor List.
The Processor shall promptly notify the Company of any security incident affecting PII or MNPI and cooperate with investigations; no external communications may be made without Company approval to avoid inadvertent Reg FD violations.
Governance and audit: the Processor will train personnel on MNPI/PII handling, support audits on 30 days’ notice, and maintain records of processing to evidence compliance.
